title: coffea boiviniana subsp. drakei
intro: Coffea boiviniana subsp. drakei is a plant species within the coffee family (Rubiaceae) that is native to Madagascar and is a subspecies of Coffea boiviniana. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of caffeine and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Coffea boiviniana subsp. drakei include its ability to act as a stimulant, improve cognitive function, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas and tonics that are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
